reviewCadence: Review quarterly or whenever the active IDE/model provider changes model availability.
principle: Select model depth by agent responsibility. Keep role behavior in the roster and skills; keep tool-specific model names in dated adapter setup notes.

profiles.label: Fast Targeted
profiles.intent: Use for narrow edits, doc cleanup, simple command interpretation, and low-risk repeated checks.

profiles.label: Balanced Reasoning
profiles.intent: Use for planning, normal implementation, review synthesis, and tasks where speed and judgment both matter.

profiles.label: Coding Large Context
profiles.intent: Use for multi-file Next.js implementation, Server Component boundaries, form flows, and repo-wide code changes.

profiles.label: Deep Reasoning Large Context
profiles.intent: Use for architecture, cross-layer changes, migrations, security-sensitive work, and ambiguous decisions with high blast radius.

profiles.label: Creative Vision Large Context
profiles.intent: Use for frontend creative direction, screenshot critique, content-first design, distinctiveness benchmarking, and product-quality review.

agentRoutes.notes: Escalate when the plan changes architecture, auth, data model, release workflow, or public package behavior.
Use for core-change council review and cross-layer decisions.
Use high reasoning for schema, RLS, migration ordering, rollback, and authorization boundaries.
Escalate when server/client boundaries, caching, auth, or data mutation behavior is uncertain.
Use with screenshots, reference sets, content inventory, and product-specific design evidence.
Use high reasoning for OWASP, IDOR, SSRF, injection, broken auth, dependency, secret, and release-risk review.
Escalate for flaky tests, concurrency, auth flows, visual regression, or hard-to-reproduce bugs.
Use balanced reasoning when docs encode decisions, migration order, or security-sensitive setup.
Escalate for release gates, rollback paths, env var review, logs, or production incidents.

toolSurfaces.modelSelection: Default model in config.toml, temporary /model or --model selection, and optional custom-agent model/model_reasoning_effort fields
Subagent frontmatter can document or select model where supported by the installed Claude Code version
Use Cursor model picker or team setting; project rules advise which profile to pick but do not force the picker
Use Copilot chat or coding-agent model selection where available; repo instructions advise but do not force model selection

updatePolicy: Treat model names as dated recommendations, not permanent product truth.
Do not store API keys, tokens, billing data, or private model entitlement notes in model-routing files.
When model availability changes, update adapter examples, MODEL_ROUTING.md, DECISIONS.md, and audit tests in the same change.
If an IDE cannot enforce per-agent model choice, record that limitation in ASSISTANT_ADAPTERS.md instead of pretending the package can force it.
